mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of structural labor shortages.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in real personal income and a deceleration in core inflation indicates robust consumer purchasing power without monetary tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or the interaction term shows zero incremental predictive value over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production signals strengthening corporate output and supply chain normalization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: The signal reverses to negative returns when FEDFUNDS changes by more than 25 basis points in a single month, or the 5-day forward return correlation drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of declining housing starts indicate a contracting construction sector and weakening consumer confidence, which transmits to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of cases following three-month housing start declines, or the signal is completely offset by concurrent fiscal stimulus announcements.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows, or inflation spikes consistently coincide with positive equity returns due to supply-side reflation trades.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: The GDP growth signal fails to predict positive returns when quarterly revisions exceed 0.5% downward, or forward returns turn negative during stagflationary periods.
